Title: Create a New User
Preconditions: Database is initialized and empty.
Test Data:
- Valid username: "testuser"
- Valid password: "password123"
- Valid email: "test@example.com"
Test Steps:
1. Call the createUser method with the provided username, password, and email.
2. Verify that the user is added to the database.
Expected Result:
- User is successfully created and stored in the database.
- A confirmation message is returned indicating successful creation.

Title: Modify Existing User
Preconditions: A user exists in the database.
Test Data:
- Existing username: "testuser"
- New password: "newpassword123"
- New email: "newemail@example.com"
Test Steps:
1. Call the modifyUser method with the existing username and new password and email.
2. Verify that the user details are updated in the database.
Expected Result:
- User details are successfully updated in the database.
- A confirmation message is returned indicating successful modification.

Title: Retrieve All Users
Preconditions: At least one user exists in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getUsers method.
2. Verify that the list of users is returned.
Expected Result:
- A list of all users is returned.
- The list contains at least one user.

Title: Retrieve Non-Existent User
Preconditions: No users exist in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getUsers method with the invalid username.
2. Verify that no user is found.
Expected Result: - An error message is returned indicating that the user does not exist.

Title: Log In with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: A user exists in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the log_In method with the valid username and password.
2. Verify that the user is authenticated.
Expected Result:
- User is successfully authenticated.
- A confirmation message is returned indicating successful login.

Title: Log In with Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: A user exists in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the log_In method with the invalid username and password.
2. Verify that the user is not authenticated.
Expected Result:
- User authentication fails.
- An error message is returned indicating incorrect credentials.

Title: Get Signalizations
Preconditions: Signalizations exist in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getSegnalazioni method from the Application.
2. Forward the request to the Signal Management System.
3. Forward the request to the Data Management System.
4. Query the Database for signalizations.
5. Return the list of signalizations to the Application.
Expected Result:
- The list of signalizations is successfully retrieved from the database.
- The list is displayed in the Application.

Title: Display Null Signalizations
Preconditions: No signalizations exist in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getSegnalazioni method from the Application.
2. Forward the request to the Signal Management System.
3. Forward the request to the Data Management System.
4. Query the Database for signalizations.
5. Return a null list to the Application.
Expected Result:
- A null list is returned to the Application.
- An appropriate message is displayed indicating no signalizations found.

Title: Set Calendar
Preconditions: Calendar data can be set in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the setCalendario method with the valid calendar data.
2. Forward the request to the Calendar Management System.
3. Query the Database for the new calendar.
4. Forward the outcome back to the Application.
Expected Result:
- The calendar data is successfully set in the database.
- The outcome is forwarded back to the Application.

Title: Get Calendar
Preconditions: Calendar data exists in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getCalendario method from the Application.
2. Forward the request to the Calendar Management System.
3. Forward the request to the Data Management System.
4. Query the Database for the calendar.
5. Return the calendar data to the Application.
Expected Result:
- The calendar data is successfully retrieved from the database.
- The calendar data is displayed in the Application.

Title: Get Null Calendar
Preconditions: No calendar data exists in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getCalendario method from the Application.
2. Forward the request to the Calendar Management System.
3. Forward the request to the Data Management System.
4. Query the Database for the calendar.
5. Return a null value to the Application.

Title: Get Trains
Preconditions: Train data exists in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getTrains method with the valid calendar data.
2. Forward the request to the Data Management System.
3. Query the Database for the trains.
4. Return the list of trains to the Application.
Expected Result:
- The list of trains is successfully retrieved from the database.
- The list is displayed in the Application.

Title: Get Null Trains
Preconditions: No train data exists in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getTrains method with the valid calendar data.
2. Forward the request to the Data Management System.
3. Query the Database for the trains.
4. Return a null list to the Application.

Title: Delete Preferred Train
Preconditions: A preferred train exists in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the deletePrefTrain method with the valid train ID.
2. Forward the request to the Data Management System.
3. Query the Database to delete the preferred train.
4. Return the outcome to the Application.
Expected Result:
- The preferred train is successfully deleted from the database.
- The outcome is forwarded back to the Application.

Title: Show Notification for Train Variations
Preconditions: Train variations exist in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getVariazioni method from the Application.
2. Process the train variations in the Variation Management System.
3. Show notifications based on the train variations.
Expected Result:
- Notifications are shown based on the train variations.
- The notifications are displayed in the Application.

Title: Show Null Notification for Train Variations
Preconditions: No train variations exist in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getVariazioni method from the Application.
2. Process the train variations in the Variation Management System.
3. Show no notifications since there are no variations.
Expected Result:
- No notifications are shown.
- An appropriate message is displayed indicating no variations found.

Title: Check User Login Status
Preconditions: A user is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Call the checkIfLogin method from the Application.
2. Verify that the user is logged in.
Expected Result:
- The user is confirmed to be logged in.
- A confirmation message is returned indicating the user is logged in.

Title: Check User Not Logged In
Preconditions: No user is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Call the checkIfLogin method from the Application.
2. Verify that the user is not logged in.
Expected Result:
- The user is confirmed to not be logged in.
- An error message is returned indicating the user is not logged in.

Title: Register and Log In User
Preconditions: No user exists in the database.
Test Data:
- Valid username: "newuser"
- Valid password: "password123"
- Valid email: "newuser@example.com"
Test Steps:
1. Call the registerAndLogin method with the valid username, password, and email.
2. Verify that the user is registered and logged in.
Expected Result:
- The user is successfully registered and logged in.
- A confirmation message is returned indicating successful registration and login.

Title: Log Out User
Preconditions: A user is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Call the logOut method from the Application.
2. Verify that the user is logged out.
Expected Result:
- The user is successfully logged out.
- A confirmation message is returned indicating successful logout.

Title: Get Current User
Preconditions: A user is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getCurrentUser method from the Application.
2. Verify that the current user is returned.
Expected Result:
- The current user is successfully retrieved.
- The user details are returned.

Title: Get Null Current User
Preconditions: No user is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Call the getCurrentUser method from the Application.
2. Verify that no user is returned.
Expected Result:
- No user is returned.
- An error message is returned indicating no user is logged in.
